# Recall

> Recall is a free, self-hostable spaced repetition learning app. Paste anything you learn — text, links, PDFs, markdown, or code — with a title and the date you learned it, and Recall uses the FSRS algorithm to schedule the optimal moment to review it. Capture takes under 10 seconds; review under 30. The whole product runs on free tiers (Vercel + Neon + Clerk + Resend) and is open source.

Recall is the simplest way to remember everything you read, watch, or build. It is designed as a calm "second brain" — a free Anki alternative that anyone can self-host. Optional AI features (powered by your own Anthropic Claude API key, encrypted at rest) generate summaries, flashcard Q&A, and grade typed answers during review.

## Live site

- Homepage: https://recall-forever.vercel.app/
- Sign up: https://recall-forever.vercel.app/sign-up
- Sign in: https://recall-forever.vercel.app/sign-in

## Core concepts

- **Item**: a single thing the user wants to remember. Content type is auto-detected (TEXT, LINK, PDF, MARKDOWN, CODE).
- **Review mode**: per-item choice of FLIP, TYPE, RECALL_BUTTONS, or AI_QUIZ.
- **FSRS**: Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler — the modern algorithm that outperforms SuperMemo's SM-2 by scheduling reviews based on the user's individual forgetting curve.
- **Rating**: each review the user picks Again / Hard / Good / Easy (1–4). FSRS updates stability + difficulty + due date.
- **Streak / heatmap / forecast**: motivational stats. The forecast tells the user their upcoming review load for the next 30 days.

## Features

- Spaced repetition scheduling via FSRS (`ts-fsrs` package).
- Smart capture: textarea detects URLs, PDF links, markdown, and code fences. OpenGraph link previews and PDF text extraction happen server-side.
- AI assist (per item, opt-in): Claude generates a one-line summary, 3-5 key concepts, and 3 flashcard Q&A on save; can grade typed answers during review.
- PWA with share target, so any URL or text shared from a phone share sheet becomes a draft item.
- Offline review queue (service worker stale-while-revalidate).
- Web push notifications (VAPID) + daily email digest (Resend) — at most one per channel per day, never sent when zero items are due.
- Tags, full-text search across title + content + extracted PDF text, library filters (due / overdue / new / recent).
- Stats: 30-day retention rate, current + longest streak, GitHub-style year heatmap, 30-day due-load forecast.
- Random refresher: surfaces a non-due item older than 30 days to combat interference.
- Import (paste lines) + export (full JSON, tab-separated Anki-importable file).
- Cram mode: rotate through a tag's items without updating FSRS state.
- Dark mode, mobile-first, full keyboard shortcuts (`?` cheatsheet, 1–4 to rate, space to reveal).
- Author's own Anthropic API key, AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest.

## Stack

- Next.js 16 (App Router) + TypeScript strict
- Tailwind v4
- Neon Postgres + Prisma 7 (driver adapter — no native engine binary required at runtime)
- Clerk auth
- `ts-fsrs` for FSRS scheduling
- Resend for email, web-push for VAPID push
- Anthropic Claude (`claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` default model) for AI features
- Vercel Hobby (cron + deploy)

## Free tier limits respected

- Vercel Hobby: 1 daily cron, 100 GB-hours
- Neon Postgres: 0.5 GB
- Clerk: 10,000 MAU
- Resend: 3,000 emails/month, 100/day
- Anthropic: user-supplied key

## Why use Recall instead of Anki / Quizlet / Mochi / RemNote

- **Free forever.** No paid tier. Self-hostable on free infrastructure.
- **Friction-free capture.** Most spaced repetition tools demand a card-format. Recall takes anything — pasting a URL is enough.
- **Modern algorithm.** FSRS adapts to your forgetting curve far better than SuperMemo's SM-2 (still used by classic Anki).
- **AI optional, never required.** The app works without AI; AI just adds nicer cards.
- **Mobile-first PWA.** Add anything from your phone's share sheet, review on the subway.
- **Your data.** Open source, exportable to JSON and Anki, encrypted secrets at rest.
